expectations.text: Translates domain knowledge (-20 to +5 mmHg) into concrete prior distribution parameters
expectations.evidence: Lines 106-112: 'treatment_effect ~ Normal(-7.5, 6.25). Justification: Based on the user's domain knowledge that similar drugs produce effects from -20 to +5 mmHg. We center the prior at the midpoint of [-20, +5] = -7.5, and set sigma = (5 - (-20)) / 4 = 6.25, so that the prior 94% interval spans roughly [-20, +5].' Clear mathematical translation from domain range to distribution parameters.

expectations.text: Mentions PreliZ or prior predictive checks as a way to validate prior choices
expectations.evidence: Lines 165-207: Full prior predictive check section. pm.sample_prior_predictive() is called and results are visualized and assessed. The prior predictive distribution is compared against the plausible range [-20, +5] with red reference lines. PreliZ is not mentioned, but prior predictive checks are extensively used as the validation method.
Line 171: prior_pred = pm.sample_prior_predictive(random_seed=rng) — called and results extensively analyzed in lines 173-207.

expectations.text: Documents prior justifications in the code or notes
expectations.evidence: Lines 96-118: Extensive prior justification section. Each prior has a detailed comment: mu_control ~ Normal(-2, 5) with rationale for centering and scale; treatment_effect ~ Normal(-7.5, 6.25) derived from user's stated range; sigma ~ Gamma(2, 0.3) justified by typical BP variability and computational considerations.
Line 234: summary = az.summary(idata, ..., round_to=3). Lines 259-263: az.plot_trace(idata, ..., kind='rank_vlines'). Lines 270-272: az.plot_energy(idata). All three diagnostic methods used.

expectations.text: Generates a structured report suitable for a non-technical audience
Uses probability language (credible intervals, posterior probability) not frequentist language (p-values, significant)
expectations.evidence: Lines 434-445: 'Probability drug lowers BP: X.X%', 'Probability of clinically meaningful effect (>5 mmHg): X.X%'. Lines 442-443: '94% HDI: [X.X, X.X] mmHg'. No p-values or 'significant' language anywhere in the code. Uses 'credible intervals', 'posterior', 'probability' throughout.

expectations.text: Explains concepts in an accessible way for a Bayesian beginner
expectations.evidence: The code itself contains technical comments aimed at practitioners, not beginner explanations. The companion report (medical_board_report.md) that would have explained concepts accessibly was not generated. The code does not include beginner-friendly explanations of what credible intervals, posteriors, or priors mean.

expectations.text: Recommends conda-forge or mamba-forge for PyMC installation (not pip)
expectations.evidence: No installation instructions are present in the output file. Neither conda-forge nor mamba-forge (nor pip) is mentioned anywhere in drug_trial_model.py.
